fields.summary: GitLab CI pipeline stuck in pending state for over 2 hours on shared runner
fields.description: Our GitLab CI pipeline for the user-profile-service has been stuck in a pending state for over 2 hours. The pipeline was triggered by a merge request push. Other teams are reporting their pipelines are running fine. This started happening after our DevOps team performed runner maintenance yesterday evening. We have 3 shared runners configured for our group. The job logs show nothing as the job never gets picked up by any runner.

content.text: I checked our .gitlab-ci.yml and we have a tags section specifying docker as the runner tag. This worked fine before the maintenance window. I can see in the GitLab UI that the job is waiting for a runner with the docker tag.
Pipeline #44821 has been pending for 120 minutes. Automatic escalation triggered.

Found the root cause. During yesterday's runner maintenance, the docker tag was accidentally removed from all 3 shared runners. The runners are active and healthy but they no longer match the docker tag your pipeline is requesting. I have re-added the docker tag to all 3 runners under Settings > CI/CD > Runners. Your pipeline should now be picked up.
Pipeline is running now and completed successfully. All stages passed. The root cause was the missing docker tag on the shared runners after maintenance. Thank you Aditya.
